---
title: "Examples"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Examples}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", fig.align = "center")
```

This example uses European anchovy (*Engraulis encrasicolus*) in the
Mediterranean Sea. The package includes prepared tables and figures generated
from Bio-ORACLE v3 surface layers, OBIS occurrence records, and a continuous
presence-background SDM suitability map. The code below starts from these
prepared inputs and focuses on the `climniche` calculation.

```{r}
library(climniche)

case_path <- system.file("extdata/mediterranean_anchovy", package = "climniche")

metric_summary <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_metric_summary.csv")
)
variable_contributions <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_variable_contributions.csv")
)
layer_manifest <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_biooracle_layer_manifest.csv")
)
sdm_settings <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_presence_background_sdm_settings.csv")
)
fit_settings <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_fit_settings.csv")
)
predictor_screen <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_predictor_correlation_vif_screen.csv")
)
```

## Prepared inputs

The environmental data are Bio-ORACLE v3 surface layers. Baseline time layers
were averaged for current conditions; future rasters use the same variables
under SSP245 at the 2050 time coordinate.

```{r}
unique(layer_manifest[, c(
  "depth", "current_time", "future_scenario", "future_time"
)])

layer_manifest[layer_manifest$retained_for_climniche, c(
  "label", "current_dataset", "future_dataset"
)]
```

The current reference layer is a continuous suitability raster from a
presence-background SDM. Background cells are sampled Mediterranean cells
outside occurrence cells; they are not confirmed absences. The TSS threshold is
used as a screening cutoff for reference weights. Suitability values above the
cutoff remain continuous weights.

```{r}
sdm_settings[sdm_settings$setting %in% c(
  "presence_cell_count",
  "background_cell_count",
  "background_to_presence_ratio",
  "sdm_test_auc",
  "sdm_test_tss",
  "sdm_threshold"
), ]
```

Predictor screening was completed before the `climniche` fit. The table below
records the variables retained for the niche-relative exposure calculation.

```{r}
predictor_screen[
  predictor_screen$role == "climniche exposure calculation" &
    predictor_screen$retained,
  c("label", "vif_after_screening")
]
```

## Reproducing the climniche step

The raster workflow needs aligned current and future environmental layers.
`occupied` can be a binary map or a continuous suitability map. With a
continuous suitability map, `occupied_threshold` removes low suitability cells
but does not convert the remaining values to one.

```{r fit-code, eval = FALSE}
sdm_threshold <- as.numeric(
  sdm_settings$value[sdm_settings$setting == "sdm_threshold"]
)
selected_predictors <- predictor_screen$variable[
  predictor_screen$role == "climniche exposure calculation" &
    predictor_screen$retained
]
label_rows <- predictor_screen$role == "climniche exposure calculation" &
  predictor_screen$retained
predictor_labels <- setNames(
  predictor_screen$label[label_rows],
  predictor_screen$variable[label_rows]
)

fit <- fit_climniche_terra(
  current = current_layers[[selected_predictors]],
  future = future_layers[[selected_predictors]],
  occupied = anchovy_suitability,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  domain = mediterranean_sea_mask,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity
)

climniche_summary(fit)
```

The same calculation can be run from matrices after raster values have been
extracted.

```{r matrix-fit-code, eval = FALSE}
fit <- fit_climniche(
  current = current_values[, selected_predictors],
  future = future_values[, selected_predictors],
  occupied = suitability_values,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity
)
```

## Niche-relative decomposition

`climniche` reports four related quantities. Climatic Displacement is the
distance from current to future conditions in standardised, sensitivity-weighted
climatic space. Niche Distance Shift is the signed change in distance from the
current realised niche centre. Climatic Reconfiguration is the remaining
non-radial component, calculated from the geometric identity `D^2 = R^2 + C^2`.
Niche Boundary Exceedance is the positive excess beyond the chosen weighted
quantile of current reference-cell distances from the realised niche centre.
All four quantities are dimensionless distances in the standardised,
sensitivity weighted climatic space.

```{r}
fit_settings[fit_settings$setting %in% c(
  "boundary_quantile",
  "tolerance",
  "boundary_exceedance_tolerance"
), ]

metric_summary[, c(
  "n", "reference_weight_sum", "boundary_quantile",
  "mean_climate_change_amount", "mean_niche_distance_change",
  "prop_outside_niche", "mean_niche_boundary_exceedance"
)]
```

Variable contributions are model-derived summaries of fitted change. They are
not causal attribution.

```{r}
variable_contributions
```

## Mediterranean maps

The map figure shows the four continuous reported quantities within current
suitable habitat. The Mediterranean outline provides geographic context; land
is outside the calculation.

```{r mediterranean-maps-code, eval = FALSE}
metric_maps <- plot_climniche_maps(
  fit,
  occupied = anchovy_suitability,
  occupied_only = TRUE,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  study_region = mediterranean_boundary,
  degree_labels = "hemisphere",
  legend_title = NULL,
  legend_position = "bottom"
)

metric_maps
```

```{r mediterranean-maps-figure, echo = FALSE, out.width = "100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/anchovy-mediterranean-maps.png")
```

## Summary figure

The summary figure uses one weighted plane for Climatic Displacement and Niche
Distance Shift and a second for Climatic Reconfiguration and Niche Boundary
Exceedance. It also shows model-derived variable contribution shares and
weighted distributions of the four reported quantities.

```{r summary-figure-code, eval = FALSE}
summary_figure <- plot_climniche_summary_figure(
  fit,
  scope = "current",
  top_variables = 6,
  variable_labels = predictor_labels,
  title = NULL
)

summary_figure
```

```{r summary-figure-output, echo = FALSE, out.width = "95%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/anchovy-climniche-summary.png")
```

## Terrestrial study regions

The raster workflow is not tied to marine systems. For a terrestrial species,
use a land or study-area raster as `domain`; cells outside it are excluded from
the fit. The same boundary can be added to maps for geographic context.

```r
fit_land <- fit_climniche_terra(
  current = current_land_climate,
  future = future_land_climate,
  occupied = binary_or_continuous_sdm,
  domain = terrestrial_study_mask,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity
)

plot_climniche_maps(
  fit_land,
  study_region = terrestrial_study_boundary,
  degree_labels = "auto"
)
```
